calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Portions Of Documents Related To Motion To Enforce Judgment-Injunction And For Contempt Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-19-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Max Mazzelli, Esq. (415-395-8040) of Latham Watkins LLP for Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF CRAIGSLIST, INC.'S Motion To Seal Portions Of Documents Related To Motion To Enforce Judgment-Injunction And For Contempt Sanctions is GRANTED. Plaintiff meets the requirements of the CRC and no opposition filed. The court will execute plaintiff's proposed form of order. Order signed. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/rbu)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-22-2017 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Judgment-Injunction And For Contempt Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-19-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Max Mazzelli, Esq. (415-395-8040) of Latham Watkins LLP for Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF CRAIGSLIST, INC.'S Motion To Enforce Judgment-Injunction And For Contempt Sanctions is GRANTED in part. Plaintiff's motion has two components. The first seeks relief under CCP 664.6 and CCP 128 because defendant violated the parties' stipulation and judgment/injunction and failed to cure the violation. The court grants that portion of the motion. The court concludes that defendants have been violating the stipulation/judgment/injunction, defendants were given notice of the violations, and defendants failed to cure the violations in compliance with paragraph 1 of the settlement. The court further finds that defendants were served with this motion and failed to oppose it. Accordingly, plaintiff has the right to execute on the monetary portion of its judgment. The second portion of the motion seeks contempt sanctions and fees and costs under CCP 1218 [contempt fees]. The court denies this aspect of the motion without prejudice. To obtain contempt, plaintiff needs to comply with the charging affidavit and OSC process that is spelled out in CCP 1211 et seq. (See Cedars-Sinai Imaging Med. Group v. Sup. Crt. (2000) 83 Cal.App.4th 1281, 1286-1287.) The contempt affidavit frames the issues that are to be tried. Plaintiff can move ex parte for an OSC regarding contempt and if the court grants the application, plaintiff must serve the OSC and charging affidavit in the same manner as a summons. The court then must conduct a hearing of the quasi-criminal contempt proceeding and defendants possess some of the rights of a criminal defendant such as the presumption of innocence, the beyond a reasonable doubt burden of proof, live testimony and the right to cross-examination, etc. Plaintiff is improperly attempting to obtain contempt by motion and therefore the contempt part of the motion is denied without prejudice. Order is signed in Open court. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/rbu)
